name: BTC 4h Deep Value Reclaim v1
edge_mechanism: a deeper selloff into structural support reclaims value and resumes away from the failed downside auction
thesis: BTC 4h is nominally chop but the recent distribution is transition-heavy, which is exactly where deeper reclaim logic can work better than simple breakout chasing. The edge is not buying every dip. It is waiting for a failed downside auction to reclaim medium value and then participating only once that reclaim proves it can hold above the support/value zone.
invalidation_condition: If deep reclaim attempts mostly stall back under medium value or the setup remains too sparse to clear screen density, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A downside sweep extends too far, price reclaims value and structural support, then rotates upward as trapped shorts unwind.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop-to-transition conditions, failed downside auctions often mean revert harder than shallow pullbacks. Requiring value reclaim plus hold should avoid pure falling-knife entries while still catching the reset leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the best fit because the cycle primary lane is chop/transition rather than strong trend, and deeper resets are more credible there than on the already-trending TAO lane.

indicator_role_map.ADX_14: trend-exhaustion veto to avoid chasing mature trend

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: What looks like a reclaim may actually be a weak bounce inside a real trend-down continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but expectancy is weak, tighten time-based de-risking and bring the first scale-out closer before changing the reclaim logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Several modest reclaim rotations, some scratch trades, and occasional larger follow-through when the failed breakdown genuinely resolves upward.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: Screen stage clears density with positive post-cost first-target behavior on BTC 4h and at least one cross-lane shows the same reclaim mechanism.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reclaim entries that lose VWAP/EMA_55 before reaching first scale-out.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because the trigger is deep excursion plus reclaim, not a multi-step ceremony

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for deep value reclaim in a chop/transition major.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows enough reclaim rotations with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h reclaim attempts are too sparse or collapse back under value immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a mix of quick balance rotations and a few multi-bar recovery legs.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same reclaim logic survives on an even cleaner chop lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h keeps non-sparse reclaim behavior after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h turns too flat and the move size is too small to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but smaller reclaim rotations than BTC.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism gains density without becoming pure noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h increases count without destroying expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h becomes hyperactive and turns the reclaim into overtrading noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller reclaim rotations than BTC 4h.

long: Exit remaining 30% on close below VWAP or 6 bars
short: Exit remaining 30% on close above VWAP or 6 bars
